title: verilated.mk: Apple clang does not support -o flag in conjunction with PCH
body:
Using latest git verilator, on MacOS 26.3.1. When compiling some verilated sources I get:

```
/usr/bin/c++ -Os  -I.  -MMD -I/usr/local/include -I/usr/local/include/vltstd -DVERILATOR=1 -DVM_COVERAGE=0 -DVM_SC=0 -DVM_TIMING=0 -DVM_TRACE=0 -DVM_TRACE_FST=0 -DVM_TRACE_VCD=0 -DVM_TRACE_SAIF=0        Vtb__pch.h.fast.gch -c -o Vtb.o Vtb.cpp
clang++: error: cannot specify -o when generating multiple output files
make[1]: *** [/usr/local/include/verilated.mk:283: Vtb.o] Error 1
```

Problematic lines are here in `verilated.mk`:

https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/4b53f5f978f9a48d12c597d8cd66fec9606d8c9b/include/verilated.mk.in#L282-L286

Apple clang rejects the `-o` flag when PCH is used. Note that native GCC isn't available on MacOS and `gcc` is just symlinked to `clang`.

Since this is just a `%.o: %.cpp` pattern the `-o` flag is unnecessary. As a workaround I've removed `-o -@` from lines 283 and 286 in that Makefile; if I do this then everything builds and simulates fine for me. However I'm not sure if this is an appropriate fix for all platforms.
code_blocks:
/usr/bin/c++ -Os  -I.  -MMD -I/usr/local/include -I/usr/local/include/vltstd -DVERILATOR=1 -DVM_COVERAGE=0 -DVM_SC=0 -DVM_TIMING=0 -DVM_TRACE=0 -DVM_TRACE_FST=0 -DVM_TRACE_VCD=0 -DVM_TRACE_SAIF=0        Vtb__pch.h.fast.gch -c -o Vtb.o Vtb.cpp
clang++: error: cannot specify -o when generating multiple output files
make[1]: *** [/usr/local/include/verilated.mk:283: Vtb.o] Error 1
